mappings.rationale: Directly covers the human skeletal system, musculatory system, and integumentary system as outlined in this section.
Addresses the physiology of skeletal, muscular, and integumentary systems including muscle contraction and skeletal support.
Covers the diversity of body coverings, skeletal systems, and musculature across invertebrate and vertebrate animal phyla.
Covers teeth as skeletal derivatives, including their structure, composition, and evolutionary development in vertebrates.
Addresses mammalian integument (hair, skin glands), skeletal structure, and musculature as examples of vertebrate covering and support systems.
Covers reptilian integument (epidermal scales), skeletal systems, and musculature as comparative examples of vertebrate body coverings and support.
Addresses invertebrate exoskeletons (cuticular structures), integuments, and musculature in arthropods.
Covers the embryology and development of vertebrate skin, skeleton, and musculature as outlined in the section.
